authoritative_sources.title: Reliability Explainer
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines the FERC and NERC reliability oversight model and clarifies where plant execution support ends and formal reliability or regulatory authority begins.
used_for: constraining refusal posture around regulatory and reliability decisions

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Reliability Functional Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines Generator Operator functional responsibilities and information exchange patterns that distinguish plant execution support from dispatch, compliance, or transmission authority.
used_for: naming systems and artifacts that govern stage progression

authoritative_sources.title: Glossary of Terms Used in NERC Reliability Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines generating-plant, outage, derate, and functional-entity terms used to keep the pack grounded in power-generation language instead of generic operations wording.

authoritative_sources.title: PJM Manual 14D: Generator Operational Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public generator-operating manual that documents outage, derate, notification, cold-weather, and return-to-service workflow artifacts used by real generation operators.
used_for: defining outage and derate workflow stages
mapping execution artifacts such as outage tickets, notice timing, and return-to-service evidence
building the cold-weather evidence-gap scenario family

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 GADS Data Reporting Instructions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines outage, derate, cause-code, and return-to-service evidence structures for generator availability reporting, which materially changes closure and exception handling in this lane.
used_for: defining failure modes around outage and derate classification

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.269 Electric Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. safety rule for electric power generation work practices, grounding, and clearance controls, making it authoritative for refusal and escalation when safety-critical evidence or authority is missing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety boundary and refusal trigger control
used_for: setting refusal posture for clearance and energization requests
defining evidence gaps that block safe completion

workflow_stages.name: Accepted work framing and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Interpret accepted generation work in plant context, confirm the governing unit, outage or derate classification, dispatcher context, and required systems before any progression decision.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution progression and operating-state management
workflow_stages.description: Track unit status, derate or outage progression, and operator-owned next steps using control-room records, outage tickets, and plant evidence without claiming dispatch authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and low-confidence escalation
workflow_stages.description: Pause work when outage classifications, clearance evidence, policy context, or system states conflict and emit explicit exception or escalation outputs instead of silent progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and return-to-service closure
workflow_stages.description: Capture synchronization, dispatcher acknowledgment, clearance release, and event-report evidence before producing closure outputs and completion status.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: control-room shift logs and shift-turnover notes
DCS, SCADA, historian, and alarm evidence for unit operating state
outage or derate tickets such as eDART-style records
clearance, lockout or tagout, permit, and release artifacts
GADS event, derate, and cause-code records
cold-weather readiness or seasonal preparedness checklists when applicable
systems_of_record: Plant control-room logbook or shift log system
DCS, SCADA, EMS, or historian systems that reflect real unit state
Regional outage or derate portal such as eDART or an equivalent utility workflow system
CMMS or work management system for associated work packages
Clearance, permit, or safety isolation tracking system
Generator availability and event reporting system such as GADS
Tenant policy repository for plant operating limits and escalation thresholds

allowed: Classify generation work as in-scope, blocked, rework-needed, or complete using authoritative records
Advance or hold lifecycle state based on evidence sufficiency across plant, outage, and safety systems
Prepare status summaries, completion records, and escalation packages grounded in generation-specific terminology and artifacts
escalate: Requests requiring dispatch setpoint changes, unit commitment decisions, or balancing-authority execution
Requests requiring clearance release, energization approval, engineering redesign, or environmental-permit interpretation
Conflicting tenant policy, regional manuals, or source records that materially change the outcome
refuse: Claims that a unit has returned to service without synchronization, dispatcher, and clearance evidence
Requests that ask the lane to act as plant safety authority, licensed engineering approver, or formal compliance decision-maker
Generic operations framing that ignores outage, derate, unit-status, or common-mode generation records required for the lane

domain_failure_modes: Treating generation work like generic operations and omitting outage or derate classification, unit-status evidence, and dispatcher context
Marking return-to-service complete without synchronized evidence from historian, outage ticket, dispatcher acknowledgment, and clearance release artifacts
Failing to escalate when safety-critical, engineering, environmental, or dispatch authority is requested
Allowing conflicting plant and regional records to pass as a clean execution state
Ignoring generation-specific seasonal readiness evidence such as cold-weather or fuel-system constraints that materially change operating risk

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional outage-ticketing platforms, notification windows, and return-to-service steps vary across PJM, other ISOs or RTOs, and vertically integrated utilities
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses PJM public workflow as the operating reference baseline, but tenant or jurisdiction-specific procedures must be supplied whenever a scenario depends on region-specific timing or ticket fields.

notes: The research supports a generation-specific execution lane because plant logs, outage classifications, clearance records, and return-to-service evidence materially differ from generic operations workflows.
This lane can manage status, evidence, and escalation, but it cannot replace dispatcher, engineering, compliance, environmental, or plant safety approval authority.
